Meha Raja, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff Tamara Avila Alvarado

No hearing held.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Continued To Mar-25-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Case Summary And Other Matters Supporting Request For Court Judgment On Default
Off Calendar Dec-20-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:49 am and concluding at 10:56 am.

Plaintiff Tamara Avila Alvarado is present represented by Jethro Busch, Esq., via zoom video conference, is placed under oath and testifies. Plaintiff is assisted by a Spanish Language Interpreter Pilar Espinosa; Certification #330288
Defendant Salvador Tandoc not appearing.

The Court addresses filing and service of the statment of damages.

Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

COURT FINDS:
1. The complaint filed November 07, 2022 is the operative pleading.
2. The complaint was served upon defendant on July 10, 2023.
3. The DOES in this matter were dismissed on August 28, 2024.
4. The Judgment requested will resolve the entire action.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Tamara Avila Alvarado , and against Defendant Salvador Tandoc, as follows:

1. Damages: $1,600,000.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $0
3. Costs: $2,829.32
4. Attorney fees: $32,505.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $1,636,334.32

Counsel for plaintiff to prepair and submit proposed order for review and signature. (D505)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-15-2024 Continued To Jan-28-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Case Summary And Other Matters Supporting Request For Court Judgment On Default
Off Calendar Nov-01-2024 Continued To Dec-20-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant Salvador C. Tandoc and Defendant Candace Y. Tandoc.

Dept.505: Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" commencing at 9:31:16 a.m., and concluding at 10:45:53 a.m. (D505)

Plaintiff Tamara Avila Alvarado, represented by Jethro Sanford Busch, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference, was placed under oath. Plaintiff is assisted by court certified Spanish speaking interpreter Shannon Raintree; 300563.
Defendant Salvador C. Tandoc and Defendant Candace Y. Tandoc not appearing.
Counsel inquires with the court if the request for judicial notice was received as the document was not e-filed.
Maria Avila and Sophia Avila were present via Zoom video conference, was placed under oath but did not testify. Witnesses are assisted by court certified Spanish speaking interpreter Shannon Raintree; 300563.

Court confirms receipt and has reviewed the document and was unaware that it was not filed.
Court informs counsel of issues of the case. Issue of proof of service of the summons and complaint and missing statement of damages.
Counsel offers an argument as to the proof of service.
Matter is discussed.

The Court continues the matter for plaintiff to remedy issues and to submit supplemental declarations re: proof of service of summons and complaint, service of the statement of damages, attorney's fees and prejudgment interest calculations.

Parties stipulate to the next hearing date.

The matter is continued to December 20, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 505.
